(i) (b) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle T onto triangle P.

(c) Translate triangle T by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -2 \\ -5 \end{pmatrix}$.

(d) Rotate triangle T through $90^\circ$ anticlockwise about $(0, 0)$.

(e) Enlarge triangle T by scale factor $\dfrac{1}{2}$ with centre $(0, 0)$.

(f) B is the mid-point of the line AC.
Find the co-ordinates of C.

(g) Find AB.

(h) Find the column vector AB.

A school has 240 students.
The ratio girls : boys = 25 : 23.

(a) Show that the number of boys is 115.

(b) One day, there are 15 girls absent and 15 boys absent.
Find the ratio girls : boys in school on this day.
Give your answer in its simplest form.

 : 

(c) Next year, the number of students will increase by 15\%.
Calculate the number of students next year.

(d) Since the school was opened, the number of students has increased by 60\%.
There are now 240 students.
Calculate the number of students when the school was opened.

(a) The diagram shows a solid cone.
The radius is 8 cm and the slant height is 17 cm.

(i) (b) Calculate the curved surface area of the cone.
[The curved surface area, A, of a cone with radius r and slant height l is $A = \pi rl$.]

 cm$^2$

(c) Calculate the volume of the cone.
$[\pi = 3.142]$

 cm$^3$

(d) The cone is made of wood and 1 cm$^3$ of the wood has a mass of 0.8 g.
Calculate the mass of the cone.

 g

(e) The cone is placed in a box.
The total mass of the cone and the box is 1.2 kg.
Calculate the mass of the box.
Give your answer in grams.

 g

(f) The diagram shows a solid cylinder and a solid sphere.
The cylinder has radius 3r and height 8r.
The sphere has radius r.

(i) (g) Find the volume of the sphere as a fraction of the volume of the cylinder.
Give your answer in its lowest terms.
$[\pi = 3.142]$

(h) The surface area of the sphere is $81\pi$ cm$^2$.
Find the curved surface area of the cylinder.
Give your answer in terms of $\pi$.

 cm$^2$

$f(x) = \dfrac{x^2 - 4}{x - 4}$, $x \neq 4$

(a) Complete the table for f(x).
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
x & 0.5 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\
\hline
f(x) & -7.9 & -3.8 & 0.9 & 5.5 & 8.3 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(b) The graph of $y = f(x)$ for $-6 \leq x \leq -0.5$ is drawn on the grid.
On the same grid, draw the graph of $y = f(x)$ for $0.5 \leq x \leq 6$.

(c) By drawing a suitable tangent, estimate the gradient of the graph of $y = f(x)$ at the point $(-4, 5)$.

(d) $g(x) = \dfrac{9}{x}$, $x \neq 0$

Complete the table for g(x).
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
x & -4 & -3 & -2 & -1 & 1 & 2 \\
\hline
g(x) & -2.3 & -3 & -4.5 & -9 & 9 & 4.5 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(e) On the same grid, draw the graph of $y = g(x)$ for $-4 \leq x \leq -1$ and $1 \leq x \leq 4$.

(f) Use your graphs to find the value of x when f(x) = g(x).

x = 

(g) Write down an inequality to show the positive values of $x$ for which $f(x) \geq g(x)$.

(h) The exact answer to part (f)(i) is x = 3k.
Use algebra to find the value of k.

k =

tex:
(a) A factory recycles metal.
The mass, x tonnes, of metal is measured each week.
The table shows the results for 52 weeks.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Mass ($x$ tonnes) & Frequency \\
\hline
$100 \leq x < 200$ & 8 \\
$200 \leq x < 250$ & 20 \\
$250 \leq x < 300$ & 12 \\
$300 \leq x < 500$ & 12 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (b) Calculate an estimate of the mean.

 tonnes

(c) On the grid, draw a histogram to show the information in the table.

(d) Another factory also recycles metal.
The mass, x tonnes, of metal is measured each day for a number of days.
The cumulative frequency diagram shows the results.

(i) (e) For how many days was the mass measured?

(f) Find an estimate of the median.

 tonnes

(g) Find an estimate of the upper quartile.

 tonnes

(h) Find an estimate of the interquartile range.

 tonnes

(i) Find an estimate of the number of days when the mass was greater than 20 tonnes.

$100 \leq x < 200$8
$200 \leq x < 250$20
$250 \leq x < 300$12
$300 \leq x < 500$12
(i) (b) Calculate an estimate of the mean. tonnes (c) On the grid, draw a histogram to show the information in the table. (d) Another factory also recycles metal. The mass, x tonnes, of metal is measured each day for a number of days. The cumulative frequency diagram shows the results. (i) (e) For how many days was the mass measured? (f) Find an estimate of the median. tonnes (g) Find an estimate of the upper quartile. tonnes (h) Find an estimate of the interquartile range. tonnes (i) Find an estimate of the number of days when the mass was greater than 20 tonnes.

 cm$^2$

Bag A contains 3 black balls and 2 white balls.
Bag B contains 1 black ball and 3 white balls.

(a) A ball is taken at random from each bag.

(i) (b) Show that a black ball is more likely to be taken from bag A than from bag B.

(c) Find the probability that the two balls have different colours.

(d) The balls are returned to their original bags.
Three balls are taken at random from bag A, without replacement.

(i) (e) Find the probability that they are all black.

(f) Find the probability that they are all white.

(g) The balls are returned to their original bags.
A ball is taken at random from bag A and its colour is recorded.
This ball is then placed in bag B.
A ball is then taken at random from bag B.
Find the probability that the ball taken from bag B has a different colour to the ball taken from bag A.

The surface area of the larger container is 226 cm$^2$ and the surface area of the smaller container is 94 cm$^2$.
The volume of the larger container is 680 cm$^3$.
Find the volume of the smaller container.
